org.key_project.util.collection

Class ImmutableSLList.Cons<S>

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.key_project.util.collection.ImmutableSLList<S>
      • org.key_project.util.collection.ImmutableSLList.Cons<S>
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Serializable, Iterable<S>, ImmutableList<S>
    Enclosing class:
    ImmutableSLList<T>

    private static class ImmutableSLList.Cons<S>
extends ImmutableSLList<S>

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      private ImmutableSLList<S> cons
      reference to the next element (equiv.to the tail of list)
      private S element
      the first element
      private static long serialVersionUID 
      private int size
      size of the list

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      Cons(S element)
      new list with only one element
      Cons(S element, ImmutableSLList<S> cons)
      constructs a new list with element as head and cons as tail

      Modifier and Type Method and Description

      appends element at end (non-destructive) (O(n))

      appends element at end (non-destructive) (O(n))

      boolean equals(Object o) 
      int hashCode()
      hashcode for collections, implemented similar (just reverse) algorithm as java.util.Collections use

      boolean isEmpty() 
      Iterator<S> iterator()

      prepends list (O(n))

      creates a new list with element as head and the momentan list as tail (O(1))
      ImmutableList<S> removeAll(S obj)
      removes all occurrences of obj (O(n))
      ImmutableList<S> removeFirst(S obj)
      removes first occurrences of obj (O(n))

        cons - tail of the list
